mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in the unemployment rate reflects tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer confidence and spending, directly lifting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month rank correlation between negative UNRATE changes and subsequent 20-day forward returns is ≤ 0.02 or the hit rate for positive returns following a decrease is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals an aggressive tightening stance by the Federal Reserve that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: More than 40% of triggered events produce positive forward returns, or the average 5-day return following a trigger is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month growth rate of retail and food services sales (RSAFS) persistently exceeds the 3-month growth rate of industrial production (INDPRO) by more than 2 percentage points, it signals demand outstripping supply, prompting inventory rebuilding and future production gains that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following a trigger is negative, or the proportion of positive returns is below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion when the unemployment rate holds below 4% for three consecutive months, further declines in UNRATE signal an overheated labor market where wage pressures squeeze margins and raise recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In the defined regime, more than 55% of triggered observations produce positive forward returns, or the negative average return is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month rank correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day returns is ≤ 0.02 or the hit rate for positive returns following an increase is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% signals a contraction in manufacturing output that foreshadows broader economic weakness and drags on corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: More than 45% of triggered events yield positive forward returns, or the average 20-day return following a trigger is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and declines in housing starts (HOUST) indicate that tightening monetary policy is severely dampening interest-rate-sensitive sectors, signaling a broad economic deceleration that depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS monthly change > 0 AND HOUST monthly change < 0
regime_hint: monetary tightening combined with housing downturn
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following a joint trigger is positive, or the hit rate for negative returns is below 50% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (consumer price index year-over-year above 3%), a falling unemployment rate paradoxically signals stagflation risk as the Federal Reserve may tighten policy into a slowing economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3 AND UNRATE monthly change < 0
failure_condition: In the high inflation regime defined, more than 50% of triggered events yield positive forward returns, or the average 5-day return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
